mission.title: Install and Configure Linkerd Viz (Linkerd) on Kubernetes
mission.description: Linkerd-viz is a visualization component of the Linkerd service mesh, providing observability features for your microservices. Installing Linkerd-viz allows you to gain insights into the performance and health of your services with minimal configuration.

steps.title: Add the Linkerd Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Linkerd Helm repository to your Helm client to access the Linkerd-viz chart.
```bash
helm repo add linkerd https://helm.linkerd.io/stable
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Linkerd-viz
steps.description:
Install Linkerd-viz using Helm, specifying the desired namespace and version.
```bash
helm install linkerd-viz linkerd/linkerd-viz --namespace linkerd-viz --create-namespace --version 30.12.11
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check that the Linkerd-viz pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd-viz
```

steps.title: Port-forward to access the dashboard
steps.description:
Set up port forwarding to access the Linkerd-viz dashboard locally.
```bash
kubectl port-forward -n linkerd-viz svc/linkerd-viz 8084:8084
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the Linkerd-viz deployment to optimize performance.
```bash
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: linkerd-viz
  namespace: linkerd-viz
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: linkerd-viz
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "512Mi"
EOF
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Linkerd-viz Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Linkerd-viz release from your Kubernetes cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall linkerd-viz --namespace linkerd-viz
```

uninstall.title: Clean up the Linkerd-viz namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the Linkerd-viz namespace to remove all associated resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace linkerd-viz
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current configuration of the Linkerd-viz deployment for backup.
```bash
kubectl get deployment linkerd-viz -n linkerd-viz -o yaml > linkerd-viz-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Ensure your Helm repository is up to date before upgrading.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Linkerd-viz
upgrade.description:
Perform the upgrade to the latest version of Linkerd-viz.
```bash
helm upgrade linkerd-viz linkerd/linkerd-viz --namespace linkerd-viz --version 30.12.11
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check that the Linkerd-viz pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods -n linkerd-viz
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If the Linkerd-viz pods are stuck in a Pending state, check for resource availability in the cluster.
```bash
kubectl describe pod <pod-name> -n linkerd-viz
kubectl get nodes
```

troubleshooting.title: Dashboard not accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Linkerd-viz dashboard, ensure that the port-forward command is running and check the service status.
```bash
kubectl get svc -n linkerd-viz
```

troubleshooting.title: High memory usage
troubleshooting.description:
If you notice high memory usage in the Linkerd-viz pods, consider adjusting the resource limits defined in the deployment.
```bash
kubectl top pods -n linkerd-viz
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Linkerd-viz will have all pods running in the 'linkerd-viz' namespace, and you will be able to access the dashboard via port-forwarding. The installation should provide observability features for your microservices without errors.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add linkerd https://helm.linkerd.io/stable
helm repo update

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: linkerd-viz
  namespace: linkerd-viz
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: linkerd-viz
        resources:
          limits:
            cpu: "500m"
            memory: "512Mi"
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.24 or higher and have Helm and kubectl installed.
